findings.title: HTTP handler (200/207/401 paths) has zero test coverage
findings.why_it_matters: The auth rejection (returns 401), the 200 vs 207 return code logic, and the complete invocation flow (heartbeat → grace finaliser → enforcement loop → audit insert → return) are all untested. The test file explicitly documents this as a design choice, but it means critical behavior — including GDPR enforcement running to completion — is only verifiable through the manual dry-run. A regression in the Deno.serve handler (e.g., the failedEntries logic broken) would pass all tests.
findings.suggested_fix: Add integration-style tests using Deno's built-in fetch against a locally-spawned server via Deno.serve + TestServer, or use a test-mode export that wraps the handler logic. Alternatively, extract the core handler logic into a testable function separate from Deno.serve.
evidence: Line 8-10: 'They do NOT spin up the full Deno.serve handler (which needs a real HTTP environment)'
No test exercises the path: RETENTION_TICK_SECRET mismatch → 401
No test verifies: all clean → HTTP 200

findings.title: No test for null created_at objects in exports bucket purge
findings.why_it_matters: The purge logic skips objects with null created_at (documented in code comment). If Supabase Storage returns null for created_at on some objects (which can happen on older objects), they will never be purged. There is no test confirming this skip behavior is intentional and correct — a future change might accidentally delete null-created_at objects or change the skip logic.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test case in the purge logic tests: listResult includes an object with created_at: null → object is NOT included in pathsToRemove.
evidence: Line 187-189 of index.ts: const createdAt = obj.created_at ? new Date(obj.created_at) : null; if (createdAt && createdAt < cutoff)
Mock type at line 33 of tick.test.ts forces created_at to be a string, preventing null test

residual_risks: The grace finaliser (listUsers loop, hardDeleteUser, sendEmail, pending_deletions cleanup) has no unit tests in this file. This was pre-existing from S03 but S11 makes this loop more critical.
testing_gaps: Audit batch insert failure path not tested
null created_at storage object skip not tested
Grace finaliser integration path not tested
